description: ExtendSim block reference with connector documentation. Blocks can be either simple strings (description only) or objects with full details.

Value.lbr.name: Value Library
Value.lbr.description: Blocks for continuous simulation - values, math, and data flow

Data Import Export.description: Enables direct exchange of data between internal ExtendSim files (databases or global arrays) and external files (Excel, text files, ADO/ODBC databases, FTP).

dialogVariables.DataSource_pop: Internal data source (Database or Global Array)

patterns: Data Import Export (Import before run, Export after run)
Pulse → Data Import Export ImportIn (periodic data exchange)

Data Init.description: Defines values needed to initialize multiple database tables and global arrays before a simulation run starts.
patterns: Place in model to reset data before each run

Data Source Create.description: Creates, resizes, deletes, and views global arrays and text files.

dialogVariables.DataSourceType_pop: Data source type (Global Array or Text File)
dialogVariables.TF_Delimitation_pop: Text file delimiter (Tab, Comma, Space, etc.)

patterns: Data Source Create (define global arrays before simulation)
Use with Read/Write blocks to store intermediate data

Data Specs.description: Outputs selected specifications on data sources (ExtendSim databases or global arrays).

ValuesOut.usage: Specifications: row count, column count, etc.

patterns: Data Specs → Equation (calculate based on data size)

Read.description: Reads data from a data source (database, global arrays, Excel, Text Files, local tables) to be used in a model.

ValuesOut.connectTo: Math ValuesIn, Equation iVarsIn, Decision AIn/BIn

patterns: Read → Math → Write (read, calculate, save result)
Simulation Variable → Read RowIn (read different rows per run)

Write.description: Writes data from a model to a data destination (databases, global arrays, Excel, Text Files, Local Tables).

ValuesIn.usage: Values to write to data source

patterns: Mean & Variance → Write (save statistics)
Equation → Write (save calculated results)

Holding Tank.description: Accumulates the total of the input values, allows requesting an amount to be removed. Inputs can be summed or integrated.

InputIn.usage: Value to add to the tank
InputIn.connectTo: ResultOut from Math, RandomValueOut from Random Number

ContentsOut.connectTo: TracesIn on Line Chart, ValueIn on Decision

patterns: Math (Add) → Holding Tank → Line Chart (display level)
Holding Tank + Decision → Feedback for overflow control

Wait Time.description: Holds inputs for a specified amount of simulation time (delay) before passing to output. Works like a conveyor with slots. Not for discrete event models.

TotalOut.usage: Sum of values currently in delay

patterns: Random Number → Wait Time → Math (delayed random values)
Equation → Wait Time → Holding Tank (delayed feedback)

Constant.description: Generates a constant value at each step. Typically used for setting input values for other blocks.

ValueOut.connectTo: BIn on Decision (threshold), valuesIn on Activity (process time)

patterns: Constant → Decision (threshold comparison)
Constant → Activity valuesIn (fixed process time)

Pulse.description: Outputs true (1) at specified times, false (0) at all other times.

TrueOut.usage: True (1) at pulse times, false (0) otherwise

patterns: Pulse → Gate SensorIn (periodic gate opening)
Pulse → Mean & Variance ClearIn (periodic statistics reset)

Random Number.description: Generates random integers or real numbers based on selected distribution.

RandomValueOut.connectTo: ValuesIn on Queue (renege time), iVarsIn on Equation

patterns: Random Number → Queue ValuesIn (random renege time)
Random Number → Equation → Select Item Out (random routing)
Lookup Table → Random Number ValuesIn (time-dependent distribution)

Simulation Variable.description: Outputs the value of a simulation variable (current run number, current step, current time, end time, etc.).

VariableOut.connectTo: Lookup Table ValueIn, Read RowIn, Equation iVarsIn

dialogVariables.VarType: Variable to output (CurrentTime, EndTime, RunNumber, StepNumber, etc.)
dialogVariables.AddNumber_prm: Value to add to the variable
dialogVariables.SendMessageEnd_chk: Send message at end of simulation

patterns: Simulation Variable (CurrentTime) → Lookup Table (time-dependent lookup)
Simulation Variable (RunNumber) → Read RowIn (different data per run)

Decision.description: Makes a decision and outputs TRUE or FALSE based on inputs and defined logic.
patterns: Holding Tank → Decision AIn, Constant → Decision BIn (overflow check)
Decision YOut → Select Item Out SelectIn (dynamic routing)

Equation.description: Outputs results of equations entered in dialog. Any number of inputs and outputs.
patterns: Random Number → Equation → Select Item Out (calculate routing decision)
Multiple inputs → Equation → Holding Tank (combine flows)

Integrate.description: Integrates input values over time using Euler or Trapezoidal methods.
patterns: Random Number → Integrate → Line Chart (cumulative value)
Math → Integrate → Holding Tank (accumulated flow)

Lookup Table.description: Acts as a lookup table (x in, y out). Output can be discrete, interpolated or stepped.

ValueIn.usage: X value for lookup (often simulation time)

ValuesOut.connectTo: Create rnd_ValuesIn (arrival pattern), Random Number ValuesIn

patterns: Lookup Table → Create (time-dependent arrival rate)
Lookup Table → Random Number (time-dependent distribution)

Math.description: Performs a selected mathematical operation on inputs and outputs a result.

ValuesIn.connectTo: Outputs from Random Number, Lookup Table, etc.

ResultOut.connectTo: InputIn on Holding Tank, TracesIn on Chart

patterns: Random Number + Lookup Table → Math (Add) → Holding Tank

Max & Min.description: Determines maximum and minimum values from inputs. Outputs max/min values and connector numbers.

dialogVariables.UseLastValidPoint_chk: Use last valid point if no input

patterns: Multiple Queues ValuesOut → Max & Min → Decision (find longest queue)
Max & Min → Select Value Out (route to shortest queue)

Query Equation.description: Intelligently searches a database and reports best-ranked record and field values.
patterns: Query Equation → Select Value Out (route based on database lookup)
Simulation Variable → Query Equation iVarsIn (time-based lookup)

Time Unit.description: Converts the value at input connector from one time unit to another.

dialogVariables.InTimeUnit: Input time unit (seconds, minutes, hours, days, etc.)

patterns: Read → Time Unit → Activity valuesIn (convert stored time to model units)
Simulation Variable → Time Unit (convert current time to different units)

Command.description: Sends Excel macro or other commands to a spreadsheet application when triggered.

dialogVariables.Command_txt: Command or macro name to execute

patterns: Pulse → Command (periodic Excel macro execution)
Decision → Command (conditional Excel update)

Display Value.description: Displays the value at input connector on each simulation step. Useful for debugging.
patterns: Equation → Display Value → Math (debug intermediate values)
Queue ValuesOut → Display Value (monitor queue length)

Notify.description: Notifies user when an event occurs (play sound, stop simulation, or query user for new input).

dialogVariables.BlockBehavior_pop: Behavior: Play sound, Stop simulation, or Prompt user

patterns: Decision → Notify (alert when condition met)
Holding Tank → Decision → Notify (alert on overflow)

Catch Value.description: Outputs values thrown from a Throw Value block without using connectors.

ValueOut.usage: Received value from matching Throw Value

patterns: Throw Value (in H-block A) → Catch Value (in H-block B)

Select Value In.description: Selects output value from one of its inputs according to select connector. Up to 253 inputs.

SelectIn.usage: Index (0-based) for which input to select

Select Value Out.description: Sends input value to one of its outputs according to select connector. Up to 253 outputs.

ValuesOut.usage: Outputs - value sent to selected one

SelectIn.usage: Index (0-based) for which output to use

Throw Value.description: Throws a value to one or more catch blocks without using connectors.

ValueIn.usage: Value to send to Catch Value blocks

patterns: Queue ValuesOut → Throw Value (send queue length between H-blocks)

Clear Statistics.description: Clears statistics in various blocks at a specific time or event. Useful for reducing warm-up effects.
patterns: Clear Statistics at time 100 (remove warm-up bias)
Pulse → Clear Statistics ClearIn (periodic reset)

Mean & Variance.description: Calculates mean, variance, and standard deviation of values input during simulation.
patterns: Information valuesOut → Mean & Variance (average cycle time)

Item.lbr.name: Item Library
Item.lbr.description: Blocks for discrete event simulation - items, queues, activities, resources

Activity.description: Holds one or more items and passes them out based on process time and arrival time.

ItemIn.connectTo: ItemOut from Queue, Select Item Out, or Create

ItemOut.connectTo: ItemIn on Resource Pool Release, Queue, Exit, or next Activity

patterns: Queue → Activity → Exit (simple process)
Queue → Activity → Resource Pool Release → Exit (with resources)
Random Number → Activity valuesIn (random process time)

Convey Item.description: Behaves as a conveyor (accumulating or non-accumulating) that moves items between locations.

ValuesOut.usage: Statistics: count on conveyor, throughput time

patterns: Activity → Convey Item → Exit (transport between stations)
Multiple Convey Items in series for longer transport distances

Transport.description: Transports items from one point to another based on distance and speed information.
patterns: Batch → Transport → Unbatch (move batches between locations)
Resource Item (AGV) + Transport (material handling)

Workstation.description: Behaves as a workstation with both processing and queueing aspects. Combines Queue and Activity functionality in one block with built-in resource handling.
patterns: Create → Workstation → Exit (simple one-block process)
Workstation with resource table for multi-server station

Batch.description: Joins items from several sources as a single item. Useful for synchronizing resources and kitting.

ItemsIn.usage: Items to batch together (one per input)

patterns: Queue + Resource Item → Batch → Activity (assembly)
Create → Queue → Batch → Transport (pack for transport)

Unbatch.description: Produces multiple items from a single input item. Used for disassembly, message splitting, or distributing copies.

QuantityIn.usage: Number of items to create per batch

patterns: Transport → Unbatch → Queue (unpack delivery)
Batch → Activity → Unbatch (process whole batch, then split)

Read(I).description: Reads data from a database when an item arrives. Supports indefinite number of reads per item based on item attributes.
patterns: Create → Read(I) → Set (read data, set as attributes)
Queue → Read(I) → Activity (look up process time)

Write(I).description: Writes data to a database when an item arrives. Supports indefinite number of writes per item based on item attributes.

valuesIn.usage: Values to write or row/column indices

dialogVariables.DB_ExpandDataSourceRows_chk: Expand data source rows as needed

patterns: Activity → Write(I) → Exit (log completed items)
Get → Write(I) (save attribute values to database)

blocks.Cost By Item: Views and displays cost of cost accumulators passing through.

History.description: Views and displays information about items passing through. Records item history including attributes, priority, creation time, and more.
patterns: Create → History → Queue (track item arrivals)
Activity → History → Exit (track completed items)

Information.description: Reports statistics about items (cycle time, Time Between Items).

Equation(I).description: Calculates equations when an item passes through. Uses inputs and item properties as variables.
patterns: Create → Equation(I) → Select Item Out (calculate routing per item)
Random Number → Equation(I) iVarsIn (random attribute value)

Get.description: Displays and outputs properties from passing items. Can read attributes, priority, quantity, and other item properties.

attribValsOut.connectTo: Equation iVarsIn, Decision AIn, Select Item Out SelectIn

dialogVariables.IfNoValueUse_prm: Default value if property not found

patterns: Create → Set → Queue → Get → Equation(I) (read and calculate)
Get attribValsOut → Select Item Out SelectIn (route by attribute)

Query Equation (I).description: Intelligently searches database and reports best-ranked record when item arrives. Can spawn new items based on query results.

DBQ_SpawnOut.usage: Spawned items based on query results

patterns: Create → Query Equation (I) → Select Item Out (route by database lookup)
Queue → Query Equation (I) → Activity (process based on data)

Set.description: Sets properties of passing items from input connectors, dialog values, or databases.
patterns: Create → Set → Queue (set initial attributes)
Activity → Set → Exit (update attributes after process)

Queue.description: Queues items and releases based on selected algorithm (FIFO, LIFO, Priority, Resource Pool).

ItemIn.connectTo: ItemOut from Create, Select Item Out, or other routing

ItemOut.connectTo: ItemIn on Activity, Select Item Out

RenegeItemOut.usage: Items leaving queue due to timeout (renege)

ValuesIn.usage: Renege time (wait time before item leaves)
ValuesIn.connectTo: Random Number for random wait time

patterns: Create → Queue → Activity → Exit (basic flow)
Queue + Resource Pool (capacity constraint)
Random Number → Queue ValuesIn (renege with random timeout)
Queue RenegeItemOut → Throw Item (handle dropouts)

Queue Equation.description: Queues items and releases based on user-entered equations. Provides full control over queue behavior through ModL equations.

valuesOut.usage: Queue statistics: length, wait time, arrivals, departures

patterns: Create → Queue Equation → Activity (custom release logic)
Queue Equation with equations for priority scheduling

Queue Matching.description: Matches one type of item with another based on attribute values. Has multiple internal queues for different item groups.

dialogVariables.SortingRule_pop: How to sort items in queue

patterns: Multiple Queues → Queue Matching → Activity (synchronize different item types)
Orders + Parts → Queue Matching → Assembly (match orders with components)

Resource Item.description: Holds and provides items (cars, workers, orders) for simulation. Acts as infinite source of items.

ItemOut.usage: Items from storage (triggered by downstream demand)

patterns: Resource Item → Batch (components for assembly)
Resource Item (AGV) → Transport → reuse

Resource Manager.description: Develops sophisticated resource structures and requirements. Defines resource pools, groups, properties, and complex allocation rules.
patterns: Resource Manager defines pools → Queue uses Resource Pool mode
Resource Manager with groups → Activity with complex requirements

Resource Pool.description: Holds resource pool units that limit capacity. Works with Queue block in Resource Pool mode.

NumResourcesIn.usage: Set number of available resources dynamically

patterns: Resource Pool → Queue ResourcePoolQuantityIn (limit queue release)
Shift → Resource Pool (schedule resource capacity)

Resource Pool Release.description: Releases a resource back to its resource pool as an item passes through.

ItemIn.usage: Items that are done and should release resource

patterns: Activity → Resource Pool Release → Exit (release resource after process)

Shift.description: Generates schedule over time to change capacity of other blocks.
patterns: Shift → Resource Pool (staff schedule)
Multiple Shift blocks for complex schedules

Shutdown.description: Generates shutdown information for activities based on time between failures (TBF) and time to repair (TTR). Models machine breakdowns and maintenance.
patterns: Shutdown SD_ValueOut → Activity shutdownIn (random failures)
Shutdown with exponential TBF for reliability modeling

Catch Item.description: Catches items sent by Throw Item blocks without connection lines.

ItemOut.usage: Received items from matching Throw Item

patterns: Throw Item (in H-block A) → Catch Item (in H-block B)

Create.description: Provides items for discrete event simulation at specified interarrival times.

ItemOut.connectTo: Set itemIn (set attributes), Queue ItemIn, or Equation(I)

patterns: Create → Set → Queue → Activity → Exit (basic flow)
Lookup Table → Create rnd_ValuesIn (time-dependent arrivals)

Exit.description: Passes items out of simulation. Reports total items absorbed.

ItemIn.connectTo: ItemOut from Activity, Resource Pool Release, Select Item Out

exitsOut.usage: Signal each time an item exits

patterns: Activity → Exit (end process)
Multiple paths → Exit ItemIn (array connector receives from multiple flows)
Exit exitsOut → Line Chart (visualize throughput)

Gate.description: Limits passing of items through a model portion, by demand or sensor connector.

DemandIn.usage: Number of items to release (demand mode)

patterns: Queue → Gate → Activity (controlled flow)
Equation → Gate DemandIn (calculated batch size)
Shift → Gate SensorIn (scheduled opening/closing)

Select Item In.description: Selects items from one input to output based on a decision.
patterns: Multiple Queues → Select Item In → Activity (merge parallel flows)

Select Item Out.description: Selects which output gets items from input based on a decision.

ItemsOut.usage: Multiple outputs - items sent to selected one
ItemsOut.connectTo: ItemIn on different Queues or Activities

patterns: Equation(I) → Select Item Out SelectIn (calculated routing)
Select Item Out → Multiple Queues (split flow)
Random Number → Equation → Select Item Out (random routing)

Throw Item.description: Throws items to Catch blocks without connection lines.

ItemIn.usage: Items to send to Catch Item

patterns: Queue RenegeItemOut → Throw Item (send dropouts)
Activity → Throw Item (move items between H-blocks)

Executive.description: Must be placed to the left of all other blocks in discrete event and discrete rate models.
patterns: Executive must exist in all discrete event models

Chart.lbr.name: Chart Library
Chart.lbr.description: Blocks for visualization - charts, graphs, and plots

Line Chart.description: Displays values over time as line graphs with multiple traces.

TracesIn.usage: Values to plot (one line per connection)
TracesIn.connectTo: ContentsOut from Holding Tank, ValuesOut from Queue, exitsOut from Exit

dialogVariables.TRACE_ScatterTraces_chk: Show as scatter plot instead of lines

patterns: Queue ValuesOut → Line Chart (queue length over time)
Holding Tank ContentsOut → Line Chart (tank level over time)
Exit exitsOut → Line Chart (throughput over time)

Bar Chart.description: Displays values as bar graphs with support for series and categories.

BarValuesIn.connectTo: Queue ValuesOut, Resource Pool ValuesOut, Activity valuesOut

dialogVariables.ScaleToHighestDuringSim_chk: Scale to highest value during simulation

patterns: Multiple Queue ValuesOut → Bar Chart (compare queue lengths)
Resource Pool ValuesOut → Bar Chart (resource utilization)

Histogram.description: Displays distribution of values as histogram with configurable bins.
patterns: Information valuesOut → Histogram (cycle time distribution)
Activity valuesOut → Histogram (process time distribution)

Scatter Chart.description: Displays X-Y scatter plot of values with multiple traces.
patterns: Mean & Variance → Scatter Chart (visualize spread)
Get attribValsOut → Scatter Chart (plot attribute relationships)
Queue length vs wait time → Scatter Chart (correlation analysis)

DB Line Chart.description: Displays line chart from database table data. Plots values stored in database fields over time.
patterns: History block logs to DB → DB Line Chart (visualize item history)
Write(I) → Database → DB Line Chart (plot logged data)

Plotter.lbr.name: Plotter Library
Plotter.lbr.description: Legacy plotting blocks for visualization (prefer Chart.lbr for new models)

Plotter, Worm.description: Displays values as a worm plot showing recent history of values.

Plotter, Strip.description: Displays values over time as strip chart with pass-through outputs.

dialogVariables.XJulian: Use Julian date format for X-axis

Plotter, Scatter (4).description: Displays 4 X-Y scatter plots (8 inputs: 4 X values, 4 Y values).

Plotter, Scatter.description: Displays X-Y scatter plot with 4 traces (2 inputs per trace).

Plotter, MultiSim.description: Displays values from multiple simulation runs on same chart.

Plotter, FFT.description: Displays Fast Fourier Transform of input signal.

Plotter, Error Bars.description: Displays values with error bars showing variance.

Plotter, Discrete Event.description: Displays discrete event data over time with 4 traces.

Plotter, DE Multisim.description: Displays discrete event data from multiple simulation runs.

Plotter, DE Error Bars.description: Displays discrete event data with error bars.

Histogram.description: Legacy histogram plotter for value distribution.

Gantt chart.description: Displays Gantt chart for scheduling and timeline visualization.

Report.lbr.name: Report Library
Report.lbr.description: Blocks for statistics collection and reporting

Statistics.description: Collects and reports simulation statistics. Auto-placed in discrete event models.
patterns: Automatically placed in models to collect run statistics

Analysis.lbr.name: Analysis Library
Analysis.lbr.description: Blocks for model analysis, optimization, and multi-run scenarios

Analysis Manager.description: Data management system to collect data and results in an Analysis database.
patterns: Place in model to automatically collect run statistics
Use with Scenario Manager for experiment tracking

Multicore Analysis.description: Manages running model copies in parallel using multiple ExtendSim instances.
patterns: Use for parallel scenario evaluation
Combine with Optimizer for faster optimization

Optimizer.description: Searches for best model parameters that maximize profit or minimize cost. Uses evolutionary strategies.
patterns: Define factors and responses in dialog
Use evolutionary algorithm for global optimization

Scenario Manager.description: Evaluate and compare different model configurations with results.
patterns: Define scenarios with parameter combinations
Compare results across scenarios

Animation.lbr.name: Animation Library
Animation.lbr.description: Blocks for model animation and visualization

blocks.Animate Value: Animates a value on the worksheet (meters, icons, etc.).

Rate.lbr.name: Rate Library
Rate.lbr.description: Blocks for discrete rate simulation - flow-based modeling

Bias.description: Prioritizes flow going through it, specifying preference for flow direction.

Catch Flow.description: Receives flow from non-connected Throw Flow blocks.

Change Units.description: Changes flow unit of measurement, creating a new unit group downstream.

Convey Flow.description: Adds delay to flow output. FIFO, accumulating or non-accumulating.

Diverge.description: Distributes input flow to two or more outputs. Seven rule-based distribution options.

History(R).description: Views and displays information about flow passing through.

Interchange.description: Acts as a Tank interfacing between Flow and Items.

Merge.description: Merges flows from multiple inputs into one output. Seven rule-based merge options.

Sensor.description: Displays potential upstream supply and downstream demand in a Flow branch.

Tank.description: Acts as source, intermediate storage, or sink. Holds defined amounts of flow over time.

Throw Flow.description: Sends flow to non-connected Catch Flow blocks.

Valve.description: Controls, monitors, and transfers flow. Sets upper bound on flow rate.

Get(R).description: Gets flow attributes as values pass through.

Set(R).description: Sets flow attributes as values pass through.

Component.description: Represents resources. Alternates between up and down states via Event Builder event cycles.

Distribution Builder.description: Creates and provides statistical distribution definitions for TBD (Time Between Downs), TTD (Time To Down), and TTU (Time To Up).

End Node.description: Ends the RBD and reports its status.

Event Builder.description: Creates event cycle classes for Start Node and Component blocks.

Start Node.description: Manages RBD behavior over time. Brain of the RBD.

Utilities.lbr.name: Utilities Library
Utilities.lbr.description: Utility blocks for model control, debugging, and information

blocks.Object Mapper: Gets information about IDispatch properties and methods from ActiveX controls.

blocks.Event Monitor: Displays event calendar in discrete event models.
blocks.Item Messages: Displays detailed information about messages used to pass items.
blocks.Link Alert: Registers a database section to receive messages when linked data changes.
blocks.Queue Tools: Views and optionally initializes queue contents via tables.
blocks.Record Message: Records all messages between two value connections.
blocks.Stop Message: Transfers value from input to output without relaying discrete event messages.

blocks.Model Compare: Reports differences between models run in different ExtendSim releases.
blocks.Block Info: Reports information (name, number, position) about the connected block.
blocks.Count Blocks: Calculates number of blocks of each type in the model.
blocks.Find and Replace: Searches for similar dialog items in the model.
blocks.Memory Usage: Calculates memory required for each block, global array, or database table.
blocks.Search Blocks: Makes targeted list of blocks based on specific criteria.

blocks.Data Fitter: Uses matrix techniques for least mean square curve fit to data.

blocks.Buttons: Creates pushbutton interface for a model with equations executed on push.
blocks.Feedback: Resolves feedback calculation order issues in continuous models.
blocks.Meter: Displays input relative to min/max values.
blocks.Model Interface: Creates an interface for controlling a model.
blocks.Pause Sim: Causes simulation to pause when certain conditions are met.
blocks.Popups: Defines custom popup menu, clonable to worksheet, usable as numeric input.
blocks.Run Model: Provides Run Simulation Now button for worksheet or notebook.
blocks.Slider: Provides flexible slider for models. Clonable, linkable to database.

blocks.RealTimer: Reports simulation run time information including execution time.
blocks.TimeSync: Synchronizes model to run in real time.
